title: French Caneles
description: A classic French dessert originating from Bordeaux, these small cakes are made with a rich custard base and a delicate, caramelized crust. They are a traditional treat, typically baked in small copper molds and served warm.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the rice flour and granulated sugar.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, canele syrup, and melted butter.
Add the vanilla extract and whisk to combine.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and whisk until smooth.
Pour the batter into the prepared mold, filling each cup about 3/4 of the way full.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are set.
Allow the canele to cool in the mold for 5 minutes, then transfer them to a wire rack to cool completely.
